mechanism_narrative: Growth in monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) acts as a leading indicator of consumption strength, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth on forward 20-day returns remains below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to produce positive median returns in expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening cycle that raises the discount rate on equity cash flows and depresses valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After a 3-month FEDFUNDS increase >25bp, the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns exceeds 60% over a 10-year period, or the signal IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, encouraging risk-taking and equity investment, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: When CPI yoy > FEDFUNDS, median 20-day forward SPX return is not positive, or the condition holds less than 5% of the time over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), a decline in unemployment signals that the labor market is recovering and aggregate demand is improving, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 6% and declining 0.3pp over 3 months, less than 60% of subsequent 60-day SPX returns are positive, or the signal loses significance in a high-unemployment regime after 2008.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and economic activity, which drives higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the coming month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month growth on forward 20-day returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to generate positive mean returns during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 2,000 thousand units indicate an overheating housing market that prompts tightening monetary conditions and a subsequent pullback in equity prices within a week.
failure_condition: After HOUST > 2000, the proportion of positive 5-day forward SPX returns exceeds 55% over any 5-year historical period, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong payroll growth and falling unemployment signal a robust labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS m/m > 0.2% and UNRATE < 5%, median 20-day forward SPX return is not positive, or the combined condition occurs fewer than 10 times in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment below 4%, further declines in UNRATE signal wage inflation pressures that lead to preemptive Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4% and falling 0.2pp over 3 months, the proportion of negative 20-day forward SPX returns is below 55% over any 5-year period, or the signal fails to be significant in historical recessions.
